rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CURTIS FORDHAM VS. CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Finra-Dr Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2025, Line 1, PETITIONER MICHAEL FORDHAM's Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Finra-Dr Arbitration Award. The unopposed petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. Petitioner shall submit a proposed judgment incorporating or attaching the award. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH WILLIAMS VS. SERVE ALL, HELP ALL, INC., DBA TEH NONPROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Defendnat'S Request For Admissions, Set One Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2025, Line 2, DEFENDANT J. PEPE DE LA VEGA's Motion For Order That The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Defendant's Request For Admissions, Set One Be Deemed Admitted. Hearing required. Defendant Pepe de la Vega seeks an order deeming admitted all matters specified in his requests for admission to plaintiff Kenneth Williams. His motion is unopposed. As the court discussed with the parties at the recent ex parte hearing, if Williams has not responded to the requests for admission before the noticed hearing, de la Vega is entitled to this relief. (See Code of Civil Procedure 2033.280(c).) The court also notes that Williams has filed a motion to continue the trial date. The motion is noticed for hearing on August 11 in Department 301. The court advises all parties that Department 301 is not the department that hears motions to continue trials; such motions must be noticed for and heard in Department 206. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: CRYSTAL BANKS VS. MIDELTON RAVYN YOKO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Attendance At Deposition And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2025, Line 6, DEFENDANT RAVYN MIDDLETON's Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Attendance At Deposition And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions. The unopposed motion of defendant Ravyn Middleton for an order compelling plaintiff Crystal Banks's attendance at deposition, and for sanctions, is granted. Banks has not appeared at duly noticed depositions. She is ordered to appear for deposition at a date specified by Middleton and the deposition shall occur by August 22, 2025 unless the parties agree otherwise. Middleton shall meet and confer about the selected date. The court finds that Banks's failure to appear at a duly noticed deposition is a misuse of the discovery process and orders her to pay $720 to Middleton within 30 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
